overrideJustification: Orchestrator override (2026-04-22). The round-3 blocker (use-auto-lock.ts still uses AUTO_LOCK_TIMEOUT_MS and does not call useAgentStore.teardown) is a scope-boundary artifact, not a regression. VAL-VAULT-020 and VAL-VAULT-021 have been reassigned in features.json from auto-lock-vault-primitives (milestone biometric-vault-js) to auto-lock-hook-teardown (milestone onboarding-ux, fulfills VAL-UX-035) to match the contract's Ownership & Overlap Resolution table (validation-contract.md), which names VAL-UX-035 as the owner of the hook-side wiring. The primitives feature's scope is now correctly narrowed to pinning BiometricVault.lock() and useAgentStore.teardown() with tests (11 new tests delivered) — those primitives are complete and validated. The hook itself will be rewritten and tested in milestone onboarding-ux by the still-pending auto-lock-hook-teardown feature. No regression exists: all 188 Jest tests pass, lint + typecheck green, and VAL-VAULT-020/021 will be validated by the user-testing-validator of the onboarding-ux milestone. Override sealed round 3.

test.observation: Jest ran 23 suites / 188 tests and all passed. Output still included agent-store and agent-init console logging plus the expected wallet-connect error-path console.error exercised by tests.

typecheck.observation: tsc --noEmit completed successfully with no TypeScript diagnostics.

lint.observation: ESLint exited 0 but reported 21 warnings: 4 no-bitwise warnings in jest.setup.js, 2 no-regex-spaces warnings in camera-kit-patch.test.ts, and 15 eslint-comments/no-unused-disable warnings across tests and agent-store.ts.

blockingIssues.description: Current HEAD still leaves useAutoLock on the old behavior: it depends on AUTO_LOCK_TIMEOUT_MS and only calls session.lock() on background/inactive. It never calls useAgentStore.getState().teardown(), and the repo still has no dedicated src/hooks/use-auto-lock test coverage proving teardown-on-background or fresh-biometric-after-auto-lock behavior, so VAL-VAULT-020 and VAL-VAULT-021 remain unmet.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Tighten the skill's procedure-honesty gate so workers must record deviations whenever implementation starts before failing tests or required mission-library reads, and consider transcript-backed validation before accepting followedProcedure: true.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Round-1 review reports for biometric-identity-vault-class, agent-init-and-store-integration, vault-lifecycle-recovery-and-migration, and auto-lock-vault-primitives all cited transcript evidence that implementation started before new tests were created or run; one review also noted skipped required library/environment reads during a test-running session.

rejectedObservations.observation: Mission feature metadata says auto-lock-vault-primitives only pins primitives, but its fulfills list claims hook-level VAL-VAULT-020/021 assertions.
rejectedObservations.reason: The code gap is real, but the metadata mismatch itself is mission-specific rather than an additive services.yaml/library/AGENTS.md/skill update to apply from scrutiny synthesis.

rejectedObservations.observation: No new completed biometric-vault-js fix feature exists after round 2; current HEAD is still the prior scrutiny rerun commit.
rejectedObservations.reason: This explains why round 3 only re-validated the unresolved blocker, but it is not an additive services.yaml/library/AGENTS.md/skill update candidate.
